text: Managing a team requires a careful balance of several key skills. First and foremost is communication: team members need clear instructions and regular updates to stay aligned. Delegation is also important—you can't do everything yourself, so assign tasks based on each person's strengths. Equally vital is providing feedback—both positive recognition and constructive criticism help people improve. Motivation keeps the team engaged; small rewards, encouragement, or showing how their work contributes to bigger goals can make a difference. Finally, handle conflict resolution quickly and fairly—listen to both sides and find a compromise. When you master these elements, your team becomes more productive and cohesive.
